resources.string: Which tag represents the main content of a page?
main represents the unique main content of the page.
Which tag contains all the visible content of an HTML page?
body contains the visible content of the page.
Which tag defines the main title of a page?
h1 represents the main heading of the content.
Which tag is used to create a paragraph?
Which tag is used to create a link?
a creates links and usually uses the href attribute.
Which attribute indicates the destination URL of a link?
href indicates the address the link points to.
Which tag is used to display an image?
Which attribute indicates the path of an image?
src indicates the path of the image resource.
alt describes the image for screen readers and cases where it does not load.
Which tag represents independent content such as a card or news item?
Which tag represents a thematic section?
section groups content related by topic.
Which tag represents side or complementary content?
aside is used for complementary content.
Which tag represents the header of a page or block?
header represents the visual or introductory header.
footer represents the footer of a page or section.
Which tag defines an unordered list?
ul creates lists without numeric order.
Which tag defines an ordered list?
Which tag represents a list item?
li represents each item in a list.
Which tag is used for an input field?
Which tag associates descriptive text with an input?
form groups fields that can be submitted.
Which attribute identifies an element uniquely?
id must be unique on the page.
Which attribute allows styles to be reused on several elements?
class allows elements to be grouped to apply styles.
Which CSS selector targets a class called card?
Which CSS selector targets an id called menu?
#menu selects the element with that id.
Which CSS property changes the text color?
Which CSS property changes the background color?
Which property changes the text size?
Which property changes the thickness of text?
Which property controls the inner space of a box?
Which property controls the outer space of a box?
Which property defines the border of a box?
Which CSS model is used for flexible one-dimensional layouts?
Flexbox organizes elements in a row or column.
Which CSS model is used for two-dimensional layouts?
CSS Grid works well with rows and columns.
Which Flexbox property aligns items on the main axis?
justify-content aligns on the main axis.
Which Flexbox property aligns items on the cross axis?
align-items aligns on the cross axis.
Which property adds spacing between flex/grid elements?
Which property allows flex items to wrap onto a new line?
Which unit is relative to the element font size?
em depends on the font size of the element.
Which unit is relative to the root font size?
rem depends on the root font size.
Which unit represents 1% of the viewport width?
vw is based on the viewport width.
Which unit represents 1% of the viewport height?
vh is based on the viewport height.
Which CSS rule allows styles to adapt based on screen size?
Which property controls the position of an element?
Which position value positions relative to the viewport?
fixed positions relative to the viewport.
Which box-sizing value includes padding and border inside the width?
border-box makes width include padding and border.
HTML defines structure and CSS defines visual presentation.
HTML structures content and CSS controls visual design.
id can be repeated many times on the same page.
An id must be unique within a page.
A class can be reused on several elements.
class can be repeated on many elements.
main represents the main content of a page.
header and head are exactly the same.
head contains metadata; header is a visible header.
section should be used for anything without meaning.
For generic containers, div is usually used.
div is a generic container without its own semantic meaning.
div does not provide specific semantics.
span is usually an inline container.
span is used for inline content.
href is usually used to indicate the destination of a link.
src is usually used to indicate the path of an image.
ul creates a numerically ordered list.
label can be associated with an input.
button can only be used outside forms.
button can be used inside or outside forms.
font-weight changes the thickness of text.
Flexbox works well in one dimension.
Grid is useful for layouts with rows and columns.
justify-content aligns on the main axis of Flexbox.
align-items aligns on the cross axis of Flexbox.
gap adds spacing between flex or grid elements.
flex-wrap allows elements to wrap onto a new line.
px is a unit relative to the viewport.
px is an absolute CSS pixel unit.
rem depends on the root font size.
rem is based on the root.
em depends on the font size of the context.
em is relative to the font size.
vw is based on the viewport width.
vh is based on the viewport height.
\@media allows styles to be applied based on device conditions.
position: fixed positions relative to the viewport.
fixed is relative to the viewport.
position: relative removes the element from the normal flow.
relative keeps its space in the flow.
box-sizing: border-box includes padding and border inside the width.
Responsive design aims to adapt to different screen sizes.
HTML semantics means using tags with meaning.
label can be associated with an input.
font-size accepts units such as px.
border can define width, style, and color.
justify-content can center on the main axis.
align-items can center on the cross axis.
flex-direction can be column or row.
1fr represents a fraction of the available space.
vh is based on viewport height.
fixed positions relative to the viewport.
border-box includes padding and border in the width.
object-fit: cover crops while keeping proportions.
CSS variables are used with var(--name).
script uses src to load JavaScript.
viewport allows the page to adapt to mobile devices.
transform allows scaling, moving, or rotating.
Semantics means using tags that describe the content.
Design that adapts to different screen sizes
Responsive design adapts the interface to different devices.
The box model describes how element space is calculated.
Flexbox organizes elements in a row or column.
Grid is ideal for two-dimensional layouts.
Selects an element by id using #
Rule to adapt styles to screen conditions
Making the web usable for more people
Accessibility improves usability for people with different needs.
Rules that decide which style has priority
For accessibility, images should include alt.
Tags must be closed in the correct order.
img uses src for the path.
There is no syntax error; the rules are combined.
In CSS it is color, not colour.
font-size expects a size; bold is a weight.
The label is not associated with for="email"
To associate it, use for with the input id.
Which selector targets an id called menu?\n\nHTML: <nav id="menu"></nav>
Which selector targets a class called card?\n\nHTML:
Which property centers items on the main axis using Flexbox?
justify-content aligns on the main axis.
Which property centers items on the cross axis using Flexbox?
align-items aligns on the cross axis.
With this CSS, what color will the text be?\n\np { color: red; }
color: red makes the text red.
With this CSS, which selector usually has higher priority?
The id selector has higher specificity.
Hides the element and it takes no space
display none removes the element from the visual flow.
Hides it but keeps its space
visibility hidden hides visually but keeps space.
What does object-fit: cover do on an image?
Covers the container while keeping proportions
cover fills the container by cropping if needed.
Includes padding and border in the width
An HTML page follows a base structure.
First the container, then the content, then the closing tag.
The form groups label, input, and button.
CSS rules go inside the block.
Order CSS to center with Flexbox:
First flex is enabled, then the axes are aligned.
First display grid, then columns and spacing.
The media query wraps the responsive rules.
The text goes inside the link.
The image needs src and alt.
Five-letter CSS property for text color.
Grid works with rows and columns.
Clue: alternative text attribute for images.
Clue: inner space of a box.
Clue: outer space of a box.
Clue: layout in rows and columns.
Clue: flexible layout in one dimension.
